revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Isobe Shrine | image = [[File:Isobe jinja - 2016.jpg|250px|Isobe Shrine]]
Torii | coordinates = {{Coord|36|24|23.8|N|136|30|10.2|E|region:JP-17_type:building_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} | location = Furumachi Ka-169, Komatsu-shi, Ishikawa, Japan | deity = Kushihikatawakeno Mikoto | status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), General Shrine of Kaga Province, {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} (disputed), Gōsha (旧郷社) | alternate = Funan-sha, Funamiyamaō Myōjin }} '''Isobe Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|石部神社}}, Isobe Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It served as the General Shrine ({{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}}) of {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Province|qq=}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} register of shrines and previously held the status of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == The enshrined deity is Kushihikatawakeno Mikoto. However, records such as the *Kaga Province Shikinaisha Kyuuki* refer to the deity as {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}, and during the early modern period, the shrine was known as "Funamiyamaō Myōjin." == History == The founding date of Isobe Shrine is unknown. It is believed to have existed prior to the separation of Kaga Province from {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|qq=}} in {{nihongo|823|弘仁14}}. Due to its location south of the provincial capital ({{nihongo|Kokufu|国府}}), the shrine was also referred to as "Funan-sha" (府南社). However, *Shirayama no Ki* suggests that Funan-sha was established when Kaga Province was formed.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0664/|title=Isobe Shrine|accessdate=2020-09-25|publisher=Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Agency}} The shrine is listed in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō* as "Kaga Province, Nomigun, Isobe Shrine" (加賀国能美郡 石部神社), categorized as a minor shrine. Isobe Shrine likely served as the General Shrine of Kaga Province and enjoyed significant influence for a time. However, its power waned following the {{nihongo|Yūsenji Incident|涌泉寺事件}} in {{nihongo|1176|安元2}}. In {{nihongo|1600|慶長5}}, {{ill|Niwa Nagashige|ja|丹羽長重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwa Nagashige|qq=}} undertook renovations of the shrine buildings, and during the {{nihongo|Edo period|江戸時代}}, the shrine received protection from the {{ill|Kaga Domain|ja|加賀藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Domain|qq=}} lords. There was a period when the shrine was considered the Ichinomiya of Kaga Province, leading to disputes with {{ill|Shirayama Hime Shrine|ja|白山比咩神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirayama Hime Shrine|qq=}}. == References == {{Ichinomiya}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|石部神社 (小松市)|version=104536857|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:石部神社_(小松市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11587231}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[ja:石部神社 (小松市)]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Isobe Shrine | image = File:Isobe jinja - 2016.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|36.40661111111111|N|136.50283333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Furumachi Ka-169, Komatsu-shi, Ishikawa, Japan | deity = Kushihikatawakeno Mikoto | status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), General Shrine of Kaga Province, {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} (disputed), Gōsha (旧郷社) | alternate = Funan-sha, Funamiyamaō Myōjin | prefecture= {{ill|Komatsu|qid=Q646898|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}} }} '''Isobe Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|石部神社}}, Isobe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Komatsu, Ishikawa|en|Komatsu, Ishikawa|qid=Q646898|lt=Komatsu|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. It served as the General Shrine ({{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|qid=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}}) of {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|qid=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} register of shrines and previously held the status of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == The enshrined deity is Kushihikatawakeno Mikoto. However, records such as the *Kaga Province Shikinaisha Kyuuki* refer to the deity as {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}}, and during the early modern period, the shrine was known as "Funamiyamaō Myōjin." == History == The founding date of Isobe Shrine is unknown. It is believed to have existed prior to the separation of Kaga Province from {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|qid=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}} in {{nihongo|823|弘仁14}}. Due to its location south of the provincial capital ({{nihongo|Kokufu|国府}}), the shrine was also referred to as "Funan-sha" (府南社). However, *Shirayama no Ki* suggests that Funan-sha was established when Kaga Province was formed.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0664/|title=Isobe Shrine|accessdate=2020-09-25|publisher=Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Agency}} The shrine is listed in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō* as "Kaga Province, Nomigun, Isobe Shrine" (加賀国能美郡 石部神社), categorized as a minor shrine. Isobe Shrine likely served as the General Shrine of Kaga Province and enjoyed significant influence for a time. However, its power waned following the {{nihongo|Yūsenji Incident|涌泉寺事件}} in {{nihongo|1176|安元2}}. In {{nihongo|1600|慶長5}}, {{ill|Niwa Nagashige|en|Niwa Nagashige|qid=Q909763|lt=Niwa Nagashige|1=Niwa Nagashige}} undertook renovations of the shrine buildings, and during the {{nihongo|Edo period|江戸時代}}, the shrine received protection from the {{ill|Kaga Domain|en|Kaga Domain|qid=Q903448|lt=Kaga Domain|1=Kaga Domain}} lords. There was a period when the shrine was considered the Ichinomiya of Kaga Province, leading to disputes with {{ill|Shirayama Hime Shrine|en|Shirayama Hime Shrine|qid=Q704702|lt=Shirayama Hime Shrine|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}}. == References == {{Ichinomiya}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|石部神社 (小松市)|version=104536857|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:石部神社_(小松市)]] {{Commons category|Isobe-jinja (Komatsu)}} [[ja:石部神社 (小松市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11587231}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[ja:石部神社 (小松市)]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Isobe Shrine | image = File:Isobe jinja - 2016.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|36.40661111111111|N|136.50283333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Furumachi Ka-169, Komatsu-shi, Ishikawa, Japan | deity = Kushihikatawakeno Mikoto | status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), General Shrine of Kaga Province, {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} (disputed), Gōsha (旧郷社) | alternate = Funan-sha, Funamiyamaō Myōjin | prefecture= {{ill|Komatsu|qid=Q646898|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}} }} '''Isobe Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|石部神社}}, Isobe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Komatsu, Ishikawa|en|Komatsu, Ishikawa|qid=Q646898|lt=Komatsu|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. It served as the General Shrine ({{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|qid=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}}) of {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|qid=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} register of shrines and previously held the status of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == The enshrined deity is Kushihikatawakeno Mikoto. However, records such as the *Kaga Province Shikinaisha Kyuuki* refer to the deity as {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}}, and during the early modern period, the shrine was known as "Funamiyamaō Myōjin." == History == The founding date of Isobe Shrine is unknown. It is believed to have existed prior to the separation of Kaga Province from {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|qid=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}} in {{nihongo|823|弘仁14}}. Due to its location south of the provincial capital ({{nihongo|Kokufu|国府}}), the shrine was also referred to as "Funan-sha" (府南社). However, *Shirayama no Ki* suggests that Funan-sha was established when Kaga Province was formed.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0664/|title=Isobe Shrine|accessdate=2020-09-25|publisher=Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Agency}} The shrine is listed in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō* as "Kaga Province, Nomigun, Isobe Shrine" (加賀国能美郡 石部神社), categorized as a minor shrine. Isobe Shrine likely served as the General Shrine of Kaga Province and enjoyed significant influence for a time. However, its power waned following the {{nihongo|Yūsenji Incident|涌泉寺事件}} in {{nihongo|1176|安元2}}. In {{nihongo|1600|慶長5}}, {{ill|Niwa Nagashige|en|Niwa Nagashige|qid=Q909763|lt=Niwa Nagashige|1=Niwa Nagashige}} undertook renovations of the shrine buildings, and during the {{nihongo|Edo period|江戸時代}}, the shrine received protection from the {{ill|Kaga Domain|en|Kaga Domain|qid=Q903448|lt=Kaga Domain|1=Kaga Domain}} lords. There was a period when the shrine was considered the Ichinomiya of Kaga Province, leading to disputes with {{ill|Shirayama Hime Shrine|en|Shirayama Hime Shrine|qid=Q704702|lt=Shirayama Hime Shrine|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}}. == References == {{Ichinomiya}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|石部神社 (小松市)|version=104536857|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:石部神社_(小松市)]] {{Commons category|Isobe-jinja (Komatsu)}} [[ja:石部神社 (小松市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11587231}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kaga Province]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Isobe Shrine (Komatsu City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
